CHANGE RECORD 
When the quality of the products change s, the QuID is updated and a row is added to this table.  The 
third column specifies which sections or sub -sections have been updated.  The fourth column should 
mention the version of the product to which the change applies.

1.0 31/01/2019 All Creation of the document Clément Bricaud   
 
 
 
 
Product name: GLOBAL_OMI_WMHE_northward_mht 
Dataset name: global_omi_wmhe_northward_mht 
 
Indicator evaluation method:  
Northward Heat transports is defined by the integration of northward heat fluxes in the zonal direction 
and from top to bottom of the Ocean: 
, 
 
𝑀𝐻𝑇 =  𝜌 ∗  𝐶𝑝 ∗ ∫ ∫ 𝑇 ∗ 𝑣 ∗ 𝑆 𝑑𝑥 𝑑𝑧
𝐵
𝐴
𝑧=𝑏𝑜𝑡𝑡𝑜𝑚
𝑧=0 , 
where: 
A and B are 2 points with the same latitude, 
v is the horizontal ocean velocity, 
T is the ocean temperature, 
and S in the surface of the section, 
𝜌 is the reference Ocean density ( 1026 kg.m-3), 
and  𝐶𝑝 is the Ocean specific heat ( 4000 J kg-1 K-1)

It is computed with reanalysis 5 days averaged ou tputs (Crosnier et al, 2001)  and given in petaWatt 
(PW, 1 PW=1.e15 Watt) 
The integration is done for the Global Ocean, the Atlantic basin and the Pacific + Indian basin. 
They are computed with reanalysis daily outputs and given in petaWatt (PW, 1 PW=1.e15 Watt) 
 
Scientific validation: 
More details  and full scientific evaluation can be found in the CMEMS Ocean State Report  (von 
Schuckmann et al., 2016; von Schuckmann et al., 2018). 
The quality evaluation of GLOBAL_OMI_WMHE_northward_mht 
is based on the “multi-product” approach as introduced in the second issue of the Ocean State Report 
(CMEMS OSR, 2017).  
The transports are computed from an ensemble of 4 CMEMS Global Ocean reanalysis at ¼°: 
GLORYS2V4 from Mercator Ocean, ORAS5 from ECMWF, GloSea5 from Met Office and C-GLORS05 
from CMCC. 
Some previous estimations from Lumpkin and Speer (2007), Ganachaud, and Wunch(2003) are 
proposed in the plot. 
 
 
Figure 1:  Northward Heat Transport  (units PW) from the multi -product approach averaged over the 
period 1993-2014 ( black: ensemble mean, light blue: ensemble standard deviation) and the 2017 (red). 
Estimates of Lumpkin and Speer (2007) and Ganachaud, and Wunch(2003)  have been added for 
comparison (orange).. 
 
Product used for indicator evaluation: 
The transports are computed from an ensemble of 4 CMEMS different Global Ocean reanalysis at ¼° 
(GLOBAL_REANALYSIS_PHY_001_02): GLORYS2V4 from Mercator Ocean, ORAS5 from ECMWF, 
GloSea5 from Met Office and C-GLORS05 from CMCC. They are based on NEMO OGCM (Madec, 2008), 
and using a data assimilation system.
